input_natural_language: The autopilot shall change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the system is supported and sensor data is good.
explanation: This LTL property formalizes the requirement that the autopilot should only transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state when both the system is supported and the sensor data is good. It ensures that the autopilot does not change states unless these conditions are met.

input_natural_language: The autopilot shall change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the system is supported and sensor data is good.
explanation: The LTL property captures the requirement that when the autopilot is in the PULLUP state, it must eventually change to the TRANSITION state, provided that the system is supported and sensor data is good. This reflects the conditions under which the autopilot operates safely and effectively.

The 'F' operator is used to indicate that transitioning to 'TRANSITION' is expected to happen at some point in the future under these conditions.

The use of the global quantifier (G) ensures that this must hold for all future instances wherein the prerequisites regarding support and the good state of sensor data are satisfied.

chosen_LTL_template: ((G (((! bool_exp2) & (X bool_exp2)) -> (X ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))))) & (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))))
